key_insight: Molecular dynamics shows that nanoparticle size controls thermal shape stability in polymer nanofluids, with 28-atom particles losing their concentric shell structure at higher temperature while 42- and 56-atom particles remain stable.
engineering_relevance: The results help guide nanoparticle size selection and operating temperature windows for designing thermally stable nanofluids and soft-matter composites.
method: Molecular dynamics simulations of polymeric nanofluids with soft nanoparticles.
Computed one-dimensional RDF and three-dimensional angle-dependent RDF (ARDF).
Compared particles of 28, 42, and 56 atoms in polymer melt and in isolation across temperature changes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports temperature-dependent RDF/ARDF changes showing shell breakdown for 28-atom nanoparticles and preserved concentric shells for 42- and 56-atom particles.
watch_item: The conclusions appear to be based on a limited set of nanoparticle sizes and interaction conditions, so broader generalization to other chemistries or longer time scales may require additional validation.
paper_title: The Radial Distribution Functions of Nanofluids: Molecular Dynamics Simulations
